extra.reason: Set local_models.mode: shadow in routing.yaml. Commit 00c285a in Clavain repo.

Server runnable via 'uv run python -m server [--dry-run]'. InferenceEngine verified with mlx-community/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-4bit. 18/18 tests pass. Changes local only — no git repo yet (Demarch-26p).
GitHub repo created: https://github.com/mistakeknot/interfere (public). Initial commit with 38 files, 2905 lines. Branch protection: configure via dashboard.
Metal worker wired to InferenceEngine. Full pipeline: HTTP → Metal subprocess → mlx-lm stream_generate → SSE. Verified e2e with Qwen2.5-0.5B. Commit 188e350.

Updated routing.yaml tier_mappings and complexity_routing to MoE-first models (Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, Nemotron-Cascade-2-30B-A3B, gpt-oss-120b). Updated AGENTS.md memory budget. Clavain commit 145a83b, interfere commit 0e9e57b.

Feasibility study complete. M5 Max SSD benchmarks: 36 GB/s seq, 27 GB/s random pread (1.5-2x faster than M3 Max test rig). Estimated 7-10 tok/s for 397B model at 4-bit. Recommend subprocess bridge integration. Doc: docs/investigations/2026-03-26-flash-moe-feasibility.md

Experiment infrastructure shipped: YAML config loader with env overrides, 8-prompt benchmark harness with tok/s+TTFT+thermal measurement, CLI with --save. 29/29 tests. First benchmark: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B at 85.8 tok/s median. Commit aed5765.

Experiment hooks wired into InferenceEngine. EarlyExitHook measures per-token confidence via logprobs. Config via YAML + env vars. Metrics in /health and generation responses. 29/29 tests pass. Commit 14ba134.
Benchmark complete: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B 4-bit — 85.8 tok/s median, 114 tok/s coding, 155ms TTFT. Results saved.
Confidence cascade implemented: multi-model waterfall with accept/escalate/cloud thresholds. 36/36 tests. Commit e39ccb7.
Prompt cache manager implemented: hash-based prefix lookup, LRU eviction, disk-persisted KV states via mlx-lm save_prompt_cache. CacheStats for hit rate tracking. 43/43 tests. Commit b15e420.

Benchmarked kv_bits={2,4,8,none} on 35B at 100 and 500 tokens. Quality identical at all levels. kv8 is free lunch, kv4 ~5% cost, kv2 ~7% cost. Plumbed kv_bits through full stack: InferenceEngine → MetalWorker → benchmark_cli.

Benchmarked 122B at 100 and 500 tokens. 49.9 tok/s median (100t), 24.6 tok/s (500t, thermal-limited). Quality better than 35B. kv8 has ~8% penalty (not free like on 35B). Fits in RAM at 65GB on 128GB M5 Max.

Speculative decoding does not help on M5 Max — memory bandwidth is the bottleneck, not compute. All configs slower than baseline. Best case: 0.8B→122B at 3 tok/step = only -6%. Self-speculative (LayerSkip) is the right path for Apple Silicon.

Created 6 autoresearch campaign beads: Demarch-9tc (LayerSkip), Demarch-m0m (TurboQuant), Demarch-4b7 (reservoir routing, READY NOW), Demarch-4wl (batching), Demarch-3uy (Metal shaders), Demarch-308 (page cache). Each has metric, mutation space, benchmark command pattern, and prerequisite deps wired.
Autoresearch infrastructure complete: training data, eval harness, hidden state extraction, 61 tests pass
TurboQuant experiment scaffold shipped. Polar transform + PolarCacheWrapper + benchmark + autoresearch scaffold. Demarch-m0m ready for parameter tuning.

NEGATIVE RESULT: Polar coordinate KV cache quantization does NOT work. Three iterations: (1) Original wrapper called inverse_polar_transform on quantized tuples — structurally broken. (2) Fixed dequantize-then-inverse with interleaved layout — 3.1x worse error. (3) Contiguous r/theta layout — 1.19x worse at 4-bit, still garbage output at ALL bit widths (2,4,8). Root cause: inverse trig transform amplifies quantization noise by 2.7x — angle error delta_theta becomes Cartesian error r*sin(delta_theta), proportional to radius. High-radius vectors (most important for attention) suffer most. This is a fundamental mathematical limitation. Kept code for reference.

NEGATIVE RESULT: Orthogonal rotation before MLX native quantization does NOT improve output quality. Per-element K quant error reduced by 7%, attention score error by 8%, but final output 1.3-5x worse. Root cause: MLX affine quantizer doesn't benefit from rotation - paper's gains come from Lloyd-Max centroids for Beta distribution. Fused mx.quantized_matmul kernel also has distribution-dependent precision optimizations that rotation disrupts.

Phase 1-2 complete: Lloyd-Max solver, BHQ cache wrapper with norm preservation, non-fused attention, benchmark CLI, 14 tests. Phase 3 (fused Metal kernel) not needed — speed is within kill condition. Quality validation on larger model tracked as sylveste-d5e.

VALIDATED: BHQ-4 produces coherent text on Qwen2.5-3B (head_dim=128) while Native-4 produces garbage. BHQ is viable for KV cache quantization at head_dim>=128. Speed gap (30%) tracked as sylveste-naj.
Shipped: routing config, generate lock, prompt cache, playtest bridge, computer-use research
Wrong project — recreating in shadow-work beads DB
Phase 1 shipped: chat template fix, thermal monitoring, warm-up endpoint, MCP stub, generate lock, prompt cache. 61 tests pass. Verified with pirate system prompt test.
Benchmarked at 11.1 tok/s (cache-io-split 4) / 9.5 tok/s (base) on M5 Max 128GB. Exceeds 7-10 tok/s target. Expert I/O at 37.5ms/tok is the bottleneck (41.7%). 2.5x speedup over M3 Max baseline.
Shipped: Prometheus /metrics export with 9 new instruments and dual-format endpoint
Implemented local provider + FallbackProvider + resolveProvider() in main.go. 15 tests pass.
Phase 0 experiment complete. 1.5 tok/s measured — below kill criteria threshold. Analysis at docs/benchmarks/streaming_phase0_analysis.md
Implemented in FallbackProvider: MaxComplexityTier=2 (C1/C2→local, C3+→cloud), SkipWithTools=true. 19 tests pass.

Completed: flash-moe built and extensively benchmarked. 4-bit: 3.90-4.36 tok/s, Q3 GGUF: 12.9 tok/s, 2-bit: 14.5 tok/s on M5 Max 128GB. Exceeds original 7-10 tok/s estimate. Per-layer timing, cache configs, and PPL all documented in results.tsv and README.
CascadeError + OnCascade + SelectCloudModel. 23 tests. Shipped in df13dda.

Wired quality scoring into HTTP streaming pipeline: _record_quality() feeds QUALITY_COMPOSITE gauge, QUALITY_HISTOGRAM (per-model composite distribution), and QUALITY_PERPLEXITY histogram. Added /v1/quality endpoint for interspect evidence polling. In-memory samples bounded to 1000 entries. 7 new tests (118 total passing).

Already implemented: prom.py has all 6 requested metrics (request_latency_seconds, tokens_generated_total, active_requests, thermal_level, gpu_memory_bytes, errors_total) plus cascade_decisions, quality_score, quality_perplexity, queue_depth, queue_wait_seconds, rejected_total. /metrics/prometheus endpoint serves Prometheus exposition format. Content negotiation on /metrics (text/plain → Prometheus, else JSON).

Already implemented: MetalWorker has health watchdog thread (_watchdog_loop), automatic restart with _restart_count tracking, CrashInfo classification, is_degraded/is_restarting states, crash_history. HTTP layer gates requests with 503 during restart/degraded. test_crash_recovery.py covers the scenarios.

Already implemented: shadow_log.py writes per-model cost attribution to interstat local_routing_shadow table. infer_cloud_model() maps local model size to cloud tier, _cloud_cost_usd() estimates cloud cost using costs.yaml pricing. ShadowEntry includes local_tokens, cloud_tokens_est, local_cost_usd, cloud_cost_usd, hypothetical_savings_usd. ShadowLogger wired into cascade accept/cloud paths in main.py.
Implemented CascadeEmitter in evidence package, wired OnCascade→JSONL+intercore in both headless and TUI paths

Implemented BatchScheduler with accumulation window, priority-ordered decode, and cooperative preemption. Components: BatchRequest, BatchScheduler, RequestPriority enum, preemption threshold, BatchStats. CLI: --batch-scheduler, --batch-window-ms, --batch-max-size. Wired into create_app lifespan + health endpoint. 15 new tests (133 total). Batch prefill subprocess command deferred to autoresearch follow-up.
ProbeHealth + CheckHealth + localDown gate, 9 new tests, wired in both headless and TUI paths

Implemented Algorithm 2 (TurboQuant_prod): BHQResidualCacheWrapper stores (b-1)-bit centroid indices + 1-bit QJL sketch of quantization residual. bhq_residual_attention() applies correction at score level (not key-level, which adds isotropic noise). wrap_prompt_cache_bhq_residual() factory. Discovery: score-level correction reduces raw inner product MAE, but softmax amplifies residual noise when applied to key vectors — documented as design decision. 5 new tests passing.
